mappings.rationaleAI: Foundational chemistry covering the periodic table and chemical bonding addresses the inorganic milieu of living systems (A).
Thermodynamics and chemical bonding provide foundations for the inorganic milieu of living systems (A).
Chemistry of living cells covering DNA and proteins addresses organic chemicals in life processes (B), proteins and peptides (B.3), enzymes (B.7), nucleic acids (B.5, B.5.a), and DNA (B.5.b).
Biological catalysts covering enzyme structure, function, and applications directly address enzymes (B.7).

Endocrine physiology covering hormone types and mechanisms addresses hormones (B.9) and general features (B.9.a). Insulin and growth hormone address vertebrate hormones (B.9.b). Neurohormones connect to invertebrate hormones (B.9.c). Evolutionary development connects to plant hormones (B.9.d).
Protein structures address proteins and peptides (B.3). Cellular metabolism addresses organic chemicals (B). Biological molecular structures connect to nucleic acids (B.5).
Carbon compounds fundamental to living systems address organic chemicals (B). Major compound classes relate to alkaloids, steroids, and terpenes (B.10).

Nucleic acid and protein structure address proteins (B.3) and nucleic acids (B.5). DNA structure addresses B.5.b. Gene expression via RNA connects to B.5.c. General features of nucleic acids are covered (B.5.a).

Pharmacology addresses drugs (C). Heart disease treatment covers cardiovascular drugs (C.4.a). Mental illness treatment covers CNS drugs (C.4.c). Bacterial infection treatment addresses antibiotics (C.5.a) and chemotherapeutic drugs (C.5.b). Cancer drugs address C.6. Addiction addresses drug abuse (C.7). Drug mechanisms cover C.2 and C.3.
Macronutrients address carbohydrates (B.1), lipids (B.2), and proteins (B.3). Vitamins directly address B.8.
Substance addiction addresses drug use and abuse (C.7). Alcohol consumption connects to ethyl alcohol (D).
Immunotherapy and allergies address drugs affecting the immune response (C.4.h). Allergic responses connect to the histamine response system (C.4.h.i).
Antibiotic resistance addresses antibiotics (C.5.a) and chemotherapeutic drugs (C.5.b). Pathogens relate to biotoxins (E.3).
Cancer drug development directly addresses drugs directed at suppression of cancer (C.6).
Antibiotic resistance addresses antibiotics (C.5.a). Bacterial toxins connect to biotoxins (E.3).

Anaesthetic drugs address drugs broadly (C) and drug action (C.2). Pharmacokinetics address drug metabolism (C.3). Muscle relaxants address drugs affecting muscle systems (C.4.b). Nervous system effects address CNS drugs (C.4.c).
Molecular clocks and circadian timing connect to hormones (B.9). Endocrine-neural regulation addresses general features of hormones (B.9.a).
DNA structure and sequencing address nucleic acids (B.5), general features (B.5.a), and DNA specifically (B.5.b).
Plant pigment systems including chlorophyll connect to biological pigments and coloration (B.6). Plant growth regulation connects to plant hormones (B.9.d).
Microbial chemical interactions connect to biotoxins produced by microorganisms (E.3).
Fungal pathogens producing mycotoxins connect to biotoxins produced by microorganisms (E.3).
Antiretroviral therapy addresses drugs against disease organisms (C.5) and chemotherapeutic drugs (C.5.b).
Historical chemistry provides context for the inorganic milieu (A). Chemical synthesis connects to sources and development of drugs (C.1).
Hormonal regulation addresses hormones (B.9) and vertebrate hormones (B.9.b). Clinical applications connect to drugs (C).
Crop cultivation and pest management address biocides against animal and plant pests (E.2).
Chemical signaling during growth connects to hormones (B.9) through developmental chemical messengers.
Cellular biology and metabolic processes address organic chemicals (B). Energy requirements connect to ATP (B.4).
